conversations.say: Morgan, I received your message about dating the pattern change. I agree to show a bounded view of the ledger pattern under our agreed terms.
conversations.offer: Bounded view of the transaction continuity records.
conversations.request: Fulfill your promise not to call any customer R based on my records.

conversations.say: Graham, you offered terms to settle my debt or pay for ledger access. Let's discuss your offer, provided you repeat what the ledger cannot prove.
conversations.offer: Limited inspection of the ledger pattern.
conversations.request: Full debt settlement or payment while respecting the limits of what the ledger proves.

conversations.say: Inez, outsiders like Graham and Morgan are pressing for ledger access. We must hold our boundary so delivery logs are not twisted into guilt.
conversations.offer: Mutual defense of neighborhood records and customer confidentiality.
conversations.request: Maintain our joint stance against outside exploitation of shop records.

public_statement: A ledger records real neighbors who paid for coffee and packages, not a script for outsiders to rewrite. Any disclosure stays bounded.

commitments.promise: I will provide a bounded ledger view to date the pattern change, conditioned on your promise not to call any customer R.

goal_progress: Responded to Morgan's message to establish clean ledger access terms.
Engaged Graham regarding debt settlement and bounded ledger access.
Reinforced neighborhood privacy boundaries with Inez.
reasoning_summary: Rafi navigates Section 2 by locking down terms with Morgan for bounded ledger disclosure, addressing Graham's buyout/settlement offers while maintaining evidentiary limits, and reaffirming alignment with Inez to protect local customer privacy.
